_comment: GEARTYPE → the StatType/ModType of BaseStat1_Value / BaseStat2_Value in the wiki's items_detail.json. Those two fields carry only a NUMBER; their stat type is fixed per gear type and is not stored anywhere in the data, so it has to be established externally. `confidence` records how each row was established: 'confirmed' = reproduced exactly against known-good per-item stat lines; 'inferred' = same shape as a confirmed sibling but not itself verified; 'none' = the gear type has no base stats at all (verified: 0/272 accessories carry one). Gear types that are still unknown are absent — the reader reports them rather than guessing a type.

_unknown._note: Present in the item table with a BaseStat1 value, but no ground truth for its stat type yet — none of these were equipped when the mapping was established. Left out of `gearTypes` deliberately: the reader flags them instead of assuming a type.

_notes.baseStatsAreNotAlwaysFlat: ARROW's base stat is ADDITIVE, not FLAT. Assuming FLAT for every base stat reproduces 21/22 items and silently corrupts the 22nd.
_notes.accessoriesHaveNoBaseStats: AMULET / BRACER / EARING / RING: 0 of 272 entries each carry BaseStat1 or BaseStat2.
_notes.weaponsWithTwoBaseStats: AXE / BOW / CROSSBOW / SCEPTER / STAFF / SWORD are the only types with BaseStat2 (292/292 each).
